result:
No worries — I'll leave it as written, which is the safe default: the doc ships "add resend + recovery" as the this-sprint fix and flags "should verification gate value at all?" as an open product decision for the lead rather than presuming the answer.

The deliverable is complete at `ux-critique.md`. That wraps the roundtable — Sally, John, Winston, and Mary converged hard on the four launch-blockers and unanimously on "instrument before you spend," with the one live debate (the verify gate) surfaced for you rather than decided. Happy to reconvene any subset of them if you want to go deeper on the explore/demo mode, the analytics plan, or the state-model refactor.

questions.question: John challenged whether email verification should be a hard gate before the user sees any value at all — a bigger move than just adding a resend button. How should the doc treat this?

options.label: Keep as flagged open question
options.description: Doc currently presents 'add resend' as the ship-this-sprint fix and notes 'defer verification' as a product decision for the lead. No change needed.
options.preview: Issue 2 fix stays: resend + visible address + change-email this sprint; 'should verify be a pre-value gate at all?' flagged as an open question for the product lead, not assumed.

options.label: Recommend deferring verification
options.description: Strengthen the doc to actively recommend moving verification to after first-run, letting users reach Home then verify before a gated action (e.g. sync).
options.preview: Issue 2 reframed: recommend deferring verification past first run — user reaches Home immediately, verification gates only a later action. Resend becomes the fallback, not the fix.

options.label: Keep verification as a hard gate
options.description: Endorse the current hard-gate placement; fix is purely resend + recovery affordances. Drop the 'defer' framing.
options.preview: Issue 2 fix: keep verification where it is; scope the fix to resend + visible address + change-email + spam hint. Remove the 'reconsider as pre-value gate' larger-fix item.
